title: Peach hand pies
subtitle: Flaky shortcrust pastry filled with spiced fresh peach
excerpt: Peach hand pies are small individual pastries filled with a spiced fresh peach mixture, crimped shut and baked until golden. The pastry is a simple shortcrust made with cold butter. Each pie serves one person and is eaten out of hand. They are a good way to use peaches at peak ripeness when they are so soft they bruise easily.
sourceNotes: Hand pies are a pan-American tradition found in multiple regional forms including the fried pie of the American South, the empanada of Latin America, and the turnover of British baking. The peach hand pie using fresh summer peaches is associated with summer farmstead and orchard baking in the American South and Mid-Atlantic states. This recipe is synthesised from the established American hand pie tradition.

recipe.freezeNotes: Assemble and freeze unbaked. Bake from frozen at 200 degrees C, adding 8 to 10 minutes to the bake time.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: The pastry can be made up to two days ahead and kept wrapped in the fridge. The filling can be made a few hours ahead.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan or conventional; high heat gives the pastry a deep colour and ensures the bottom bakes through

glossaryTerms.definition: A well-sealed hand pie keeps its filling inside and does not leak. Place the top pastry disc over the filling and press the edges together firmly with your fingers, then go around again with a fork, pressing the tines into the joined edges to create a tight mechanical seal. Leave a 2 cm border of pastry free of filling around the edge; filling right to the rim prevents a good seal. Cut two small steam vents in the top with a sharp knife to allow steam to escape during baking.

content.text: The filling must not be too wet or it will make the base soggy. Toss the peach pieces in cornflour, which absorbs the excess juice as the pie bakes. Keep the filling pieces small enough that they sit flat without doming the pastry top. Good
properly is the step that determines whether the filling stays in or runs out.

items.prepNote: peeled, stoned and diced into 1 cm pieces

content.text: Rub the 150 g cold butter into the 300 g flour, 2 tbsp icing sugar and half a teaspoon salt until the mixture looks like coarse breadcrumbs. Add 4 to 5 tablespoons of cold water, one tablespoon at a time, mixing with a fork until the dough just comes together. Form into a disc, wrap and refrigerate for 30 minutes. For the filling, toss the diced peaches with the 3 tbsp caster sugar, 1 tbsp cornflour, half a teaspoon cinnamon and the lemon juice and zest.

Heat the oven to 200 degrees C. On a lightly floured surface, roll the pastry to about 3 mm thickness. Cut 12 circles about 13 cm across, using a saucer or bowl as a guide. Place 2 to 3 tablespoons of filling in the centre of 6 circles, leaving a 2 cm border. Brush the border with egg wash. Place the remaining circles on top. Press the edges firmly, then crimp all the way round with a fork. Cut two small vents in each top. Place on a lined baking tray, brush the tops with egg wash and bake for 20 to 25 minutes until the pastry is deep brown and sounds hollow when tapped.

items.solution: The edges were not sealed tightly. Press the pastry edges firmly with your fingers first, then go over with a fork. Also check you left a 2 cm border free of filling.
The pastry was too warm when it went into the oven. After assembling, chill the pies on the tray for 15 minutes before baking.
